title: Multiclient Study: Is Linux Already a Viable Alternative OS for Small Business?
author: Peter S. Kastner / Aberdeen Group
abstract: Aberdeen Group proposes a multiclient research study to evaluate Linux adoption and viability as an alternative operating system for small businesses globally. The study outlines qualitative (25 on-site interviews) and quantitative (400 telephone interviews) research methodologies to assess Linux acceptance depth, breadth, and timing in the $20M–$100M small business segment. Subscription tiers range from $10,000 (Reader) to $28,000 (Sponsor) with a target delivery of January 31, 2000.
importance_rationale: Early analysis of Linux adoption in the underserved small business segment; precedes widespread Linux SMB deployment and captures industry inflection point.
relevance_rationale: Small business technology adoption research framework remains relevant; specific Linux-vs-Windows NT framing is dated but the methodology is still instructive.
prescience_rationale: Linux did become a dominant server OS even in SMB environments; Aberdeen's prediction that small ISPs and e-commerce firms would lead adoption proved accurate.
